articles.title: A Day with a Super Laser
articles.summary: This article is about a scientist who worked with a very powerful laser called the Texas Petawatt. The laser is hidden underground at the University of Texas and was used for science experiments. On a shot day, the scientist would spend hours getting the laser ready. They would check everything carefully to make sure it was safe and working right. The laser makes a tiny pulse of light super strong, like having more power than all the electricity in the U.S. for a very short time. Scientists used it to study stars and fusion energy, which could help make clean power. The laser is now closed because of money problems, but it was part of a big network of laser labs across the country. The scientist describes the quiet work and the exciting few seconds when the laser fires.

articles.title: Wind Farms: A Heartbreaking Change for Some
articles.summary: In Wales, people are talking about building big wind turbines to make clean energy. Grace lives near Abercarn and loves the open land with birds and animals. She already sees eight wind turbines from her window and supports renewable energy. But new plans might add up to 20 more turbines, some very tall, which could change the landscape a lot. Grace is worried this will hurt the natural beauty she enjoys. Many people in Wales have shared their thoughts with the BBC, with some supporting wind farms for clean energy and others, like Grace, concerned about the impact on their communities. The Welsh government wants to use only renewable energy by 2035, so this debate is important for the future.

articles.title: Olive Oil Helps Your Brain Stay Strong
articles.summary: Scientists found that a special type of olive oil called extra virgin olive oil can help keep your brain healthy. They studied 656 older adults who were overweight and had health issues. Over two years, they looked at what people ate, including different kinds of olive oil, and checked their gut bacteria and brain function. People who ate extra virgin olive oil had better brain power and more types of good bacteria in their gut. Extra virgin olive oil is made by squeezing olives without chemicals, keeping healthy stuff like vitamins. Refined olive oil is processed and loses some of these good things. This shows that eating the right kind of fat, like extra virgin olive oil, is important for your brain as you get older. It might help prevent memory problems.

articles.title: How Caffeine Helps Ants Find Food
articles.summary: Scientists discovered that ants get better at finding food when they eat a little caffeine. In a study, ants were given sugary treats with different amounts of caffeine. The ants that had caffeine learned to take straighter paths to the food, even though they did not walk faster. This means caffeine helps ants remember where food is. The research used Argentine ants, which are a type of invasive pest that can cause problems worldwide. The goal is to use caffeine to make poisoned bait more effective for pest control, so ants find it quicker and share it with their colony. This could help stop these pests from spreading. The study involved 142 ants tested in a lab with a special tracking system to watch their movements.

articles.title: Great White Sharks in Trouble from Hot Water
articles.summary: Great white sharks are having a hard time because the ocean is getting warmer. These big sharks keep their bodies warmer than the water around them, which helps them swim fast and hunt. But as the water heats up from climate change, they can get too hot and might not survive. They also have less food to eat because of overfishing. Scientists studied sharks and found they need to move to cooler waters or slow down to stay safe. Sharks are important for the ocean because they help keep other animals in balance. If sharks disappear, it could hurt the whole ocean ecosystem. People are working to protect sharks by learning more about them and creating safe areas.

articles.title: Greenland Ice Melt: A Story from Long Ago and Today
articles.summary: Scientists found that a big ice cap in Greenland called Prudhoe Dome melted completely about 7,000 years ago. This happened during a time called the Holocene, when the climate was stable and people started farming. The study used a project called GreenDrill to drill into the ice and collect rocks and soil from below. By checking these materials, they learned when the ice last melted. This is important because it shows that even small warming can make ice disappear. Today, human activities are warming the Earth, so this ice might melt again, which could raise sea levels and affect coastal areas. The research helps scientists predict future melting and protect communities.
